pattern: Checking whether one string is formed by interleaving two others is a two-dimensional reachability DP over how many characters of each source have been consumed.
hints: If the combined length of the two sources does not equal the target length, no interleaving can exist.
Track a position in each source; the next target character must come from advancing exactly one of the two sources when it matches.

Let dp[i][j] mean the first i+j target characters can be built from the first i of s1 and first j of s2; it is reachable when the previous state advanced through a matching s1[i-1] or s2[j-1] character.

edge_inputs.description: right letters wrong order is not an interleaving
one source empty, target equals the other
shared prefix forces a real branching decision

stress.description: construct s3 as a genuine random interleaving of s1 and s2 so the answer is true
stress.note: tiny alphabet plus a true interleave path stresses the branching DP
